description: Attorney edit-time in minutes — the commercial metric that decides senior-partner adoption. Lower edit-time is better; complemented by character-level edit-distance between original draft and attorney-approved version. Per-run absolute thresholds are calibrated against the run's empirical distribution; v1.0 reports relative quartiles, v1.1 introduces calibrated absolute thresholds.
sub_criteria: Total edit-time in minutes from review start to ship-ready
Edit-distance (character-level) between original and approved versions

scoring_levels.label: Excellent
scoring_levels.criteria: Attorney edit-time falls in the bottom quartile (< 25th percentile) of the run's edit-time distribution. Character-level edit distance is dominated by insertions of attorney-preferred phrasing rather than substantive corrections. Zero structural rewrites required; independent claim shipped as drafted.

scoring_levels.label: Good
scoring_levels.criteria: Edit-time within the first quartile (25th–50th percentile). Edits are predominantly surface-level (terminology preferences, formatting, punctuation). At most one independent-claim refinement, and zero structural rewrites of specification sections.

scoring_levels.label: Adequate
scoring_levels.criteria: Edit-time within the median range (50th–75th percentile). Mix of surface and structural edits. One or two dependent-claim rewrites and at most one substantive specification-section rewrite. Independent claim retained with minor scope adjustment.

scoring_levels.label: Weak
scoring_levels.criteria: Edit-time in the fourth quartile (> 75th percentile). Multiple structural rewrites required across specification sections. Independent claim partially rewritten to address scope or §112 issues introduced by the AI draft.

scoring_levels.label: Unacceptable
scoring_levels.criteria: Faster for the attorney to draft from scratch than to edit the AI output. Majority of independent claim rewritten; substantial portions of the specification rewritten; the AI-generated draft did not save attorney time.

track_b_protocol: Reviewer is timed from opening the anonymized draft to declaring ship-readiness. The edit format (track-changes Word doc, plain-text diff, or in-platform editor with timestamp logging) is open question §16.2 — pending reviewer-panel input. v1.0 quartile thresholds are derived from each run's empirical distribution; v1.1 introduces absolute thresholds calibrated against the panel's first six runs.
